title: Game or App Crash
shortDescription: The game or application has crashed unexpectedly on PS5.
fullDescription:
Error code CE-108255-1 is one of the most common PS5 error codes and indicates that a game or application has crashed during operation. This can happen due to a software bug in the game itself, corrupted game data, or occasionally due to system software issues.

The error typically appears as a pop-up message after a game freezes or closes unexpectedly. While frustrating, it is usually a software-level issue that can be resolved without hardware repair. Developers frequently release patches to address the underlying bugs that cause this crash.

If the error occurs repeatedly with one specific game, the issue is most likely related to that game's data or a known bug. If it happens across multiple games, the problem may be with the PS5 system software or storage drive.

causes.cause: Software bug in the game or application
Corrupted game data or installation files

symptoms: Game freezes and returns to home screen
Error message CE-108255-1 displayed after crash
Game progress since last save is lost
Console may become temporarily unresponsive before crash

fixSteps.title: Restart the PS5
fixSteps.instruction: Press and hold the PS button on the controller, select 'Restart PS5' from the power menu. This clears temporary memory and can resolve transient crashes.

fixSteps.title: Update the game
fixSteps.instruction: Highlight the game on the home screen, press the Options button, and select 'Check for Update'. Install any available patches as they often fix crash-causing bugs.

fixSteps.title: Update system software
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > System > System Software > System Software Update and Settings. Download and install any available updates.

fixSteps.title: Delete and reinstall the game
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Storage > Games and Apps. Select the problematic game and delete it. Then reinstall it from your library or disc. This replaces any corrupted files.

fixSteps.title: Rebuild the database
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the PS5 completely. Hold the power button until you hear two beeps to enter Safe Mode. Select 'Rebuild Database'. This reorganizes data on the SSD and can fix corruption issues.

fixSteps.title: Clear the cache and reset
fixSteps.instruction: In Safe Mode, select 'Clear Cache and Rebuild Database'. If the problem persists across multiple games, consider 'Reset PS5 (Reinstall System Software)' as a last resort. Back up saves to cloud or USB first.

whenToCallPro: If the error occurs across multiple games after a full system reinstall, the SSD or APU may be failing and requires professional diagnosis.

faq.q: Is CE-108255-1 a hardware problem?
faq.a: In most cases, no. It is a software crash error. However, if it persists across all games after reinstalling the system, a hardware issue such as a failing SSD is possible.

faq.q: Will I lose my save data if I reinstall the game?
faq.a: PS5 save data is stored separately from game installations. Your saves remain intact when you delete and reinstall a game. However, back up to PS Plus cloud or USB to be safe.

faq.q: Why does only one game crash with this error?
faq.a: The game likely has a bug that triggers the crash. Check for patches or look for known issues on the developer's support page.

title: Can't Start the Game or App
shortDescription: The PS5 cannot start the selected game or application.
fullDescription:
Error code CE-100005-6 appears when the PS5 fails to launch a game or application. This often occurs after a game update, system update, or when game files have become corrupted during download or installation.

The error can also appear if a disc-based game has an issue reading data, or if an external storage drive is not functioning properly. In some cases, the error is tied to a specific game version mismatch between the installed data and the disc or digital license.

This is primarily a software issue and is usually resolved by reinstalling the affected game or performing a database rebuild through Safe Mode.
symptoms: Error message appears when trying to launch a game
Game icon appears on home screen but will not start
Download or copy progress may have shown errors previously
Other games may still work normally

fixSteps.title: Restart the PS5
fixSteps.instruction: Fully restart the console by holding the PS button and selecting Restart PS5. Do not use rest mode.

fixSteps.title: Check for game updates
fixSteps.instruction: Highlight the game, press Options, and select Check for Update. An incomplete update can prevent launch.

fixSteps.title: Delete and reinstall the game
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Storage, find the game, delete it completely, then download it again from your Library or insert the disc to reinstall.

fixSteps.title: Clean the game disc
fixSteps.instruction: If using a disc, remove it and clean gently from center to edge with a soft microfiber cloth. Check for deep scratches that may prevent reading.

fixSteps.title: Rebuild the database
fixSteps.instruction: Boot into Safe Mode (hold power button for two beeps) and select Rebuild Database. This can fix file system issues preventing game launch.

whenToCallPro: If the error affects all games and persists after system software reinstallation, the internal SSD may need replacement.

faq.q: Does this error mean my game is permanently broken?
faq.a: No. Deleting and reinstalling the game resolves this issue in the vast majority of cases. Your save data is preserved separately.

faq.q: Can a scratched disc cause CE-100005-6?
faq.a: Yes. If the disc is scratched badly enough that the PS5 cannot read all required data, the game will fail to launch with this error.

title: Network Connection Failed
shortDescription: The PS5 cannot connect to the network or PlayStation Network servers.
fullDescription:
Error code NW-102216-2 indicates a network connectivity failure on the PS5. The console is either unable to reach the internet or unable to connect to PlayStation Network servers. This can stem from local network issues, ISP problems, or PSN server outages.

The error frequently appears when trying to sign into PSN, access the PlayStation Store, or play online multiplayer games. It can also appear during downloads or when trying to sync trophies.

Before troubleshooting your own network, it is worth checking the PlayStation Network service status page to rule out a server-side outage, which is beyond your control.

causes.cause: Wi-Fi signal interference or weak connection

symptoms: Cannot sign into PlayStation Network
Online features and multiplayer unavailable
PlayStation Store fails to load
Downloads and updates stall or fail
Error message NW-102216-2 displayed

fixSteps.title: Check PSN server status
fixSteps.instruction: Visit status.playstation.com on a phone or computer to check if PSN services are down. If servers are offline, wait for Sony to restore them.

fixSteps.title: Restart your network equipment
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug your modem and router for 30 seconds, then plug them back in. Wait 2-3 minutes for the connection to fully re-establish before testing the PS5.

fixSteps.title: Test the PS5 internet connection
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Network > Connection Status > Test Internet Connection. Note which step fails (IP address, Internet, or PSN).

fixSteps.title: Switch to a wired connection
fixSteps.instruction: Connect an Ethernet cable directly from your router to the PS5. Wired connections are more stable and eliminate Wi-Fi interference issues.

fixSteps.title: Change DNS settings
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Network > Set Up Internet Connection. Select your connection and choose Advanced Settings. Set DNS to Manual and use Primary: 8.8.8.8, Secondary: 8.8.4.4 (Google DNS) or Primary: 1.1.1.1, Secondary: 1.0.0.1 (Cloudflare DNS).

whenToCallPro: If you have verified PSN is online and other devices work fine on your network but the PS5 still cannot connect, contact PlayStation Support as the Wi-Fi module may be defective.

faq.q: Is this error always on my end?
faq.a: No. NW-102216-2 frequently appears during PSN server outages. Always check the PSN status page first before troubleshooting your own network.

faq.q: Will changing DNS really help?
faq.a: Yes, in many cases. Your ISP's default DNS servers may be slow or unreliable. Switching to Google or Cloudflare DNS often improves connectivity and download speeds.

title: Connection to Server Lost
shortDescription: The connection to the game server has been lost during an online session.
fullDescription:
Error code CE-107880-4 occurs when the PS5 loses its connection to a game's online server during gameplay. This can result from unstable internet, server-side issues, or network configuration problems that cause packet loss or high latency.

The error is particularly common in online multiplayer games and live-service titles where a persistent server connection is required. Momentary internet drops that would be unnoticeable during browsing can trigger disconnections in games with strict connectivity requirements.

While occasionally caused by game server outages, this error more commonly points to an unstable local network connection that needs optimization.

causes.cause: Unstable Wi-Fi connection with packet loss
NAT type restrictions blocking game traffic

symptoms: Disconnected from online game mid-session
Error message CE-107880-4 appears
Returned to game menu or main screen
Online match progress may be lost

fixSteps.title: Check the game server status
fixSteps.instruction: Visit the game developer's status page or social media accounts to check for known server issues or maintenance windows.

fixSteps.title: Use a wired Ethernet connection
fixSteps.instruction: Connect the PS5 directly to your router using an Ethernet cable. This eliminates Wi-Fi interference and significantly reduces packet loss.

fixSteps.title: Restart network equipment
fixSteps.instruction: Power cycle your modem and router by unplugging them for 30 seconds. Reconnect modem first, wait one minute, then reconnect the router.

fixSteps.title: Check NAT type
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Network > Connection Status > View Connection Status. Your NAT type should be Type 1 or Type 2. If it shows Type 3, you may need to enable UPnP on your router or set up port forwarding.

fixSteps.title: Set up port forwarding
fixSteps.instruction: Log into your router's admin panel and forward the following ports for the PS5: TCP 80, 443, 3478, 3479, 3480 and UDP 3478, 3479, 49152-65535. Assign the PS5 a static IP address first.

whenToCallPro: If you consistently experience disconnections on a wired connection with proper port forwarding, contact your ISP to check for line quality issues.

faq.q: Why do I only get this error in certain games?
faq.a: Different games have different server architectures and connection requirements. Some games are more sensitive to packet loss and latency than others.

faq.q: What is NAT type and why does it matter?
faq.a: NAT (Network Address Translation) type determines how easily your PS5 can communicate with other players. Type 1 (open) is best, Type 2 (moderate) is acceptable, and Type 3 (strict) can cause frequent disconnections.

title: Game Update Installation Failed
shortDescription: A game update could not be installed on the PS5.
fullDescription:
Error code CE-108862-5 indicates that the PS5 was unable to install a game update or patch. This can occur due to insufficient storage space, corrupted download files, or a conflict with the existing game installation.

Game updates are essential for online play and often contain critical bug fixes. When this error occurs, the game may still launch in its current version for offline play, but online features will typically be locked until the update is successfully installed.

The most common resolution is to delete the partially downloaded update and try again, or to completely reinstall the game if the update repeatedly fails.

causes.cause: Corrupted game installation conflicting with update

symptoms: Update download completes but installation fails
Error message CE-108862-5 appears
Game may revert to requiring the update again
Online features locked until update installed

fixSteps.title: Delete the failed update
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Storage > Games and Apps. Find the game and select it. Look for the update file and delete it, then re-download the update.

fixSteps.title: Free up storage space
fixSteps.instruction: The PS5 needs additional free space beyond the update size for installation. Go to Settings > Storage and delete unused games or media to free at least 50GB.

fixSteps.title: Restart the PS5 and retry
fixSteps.instruction: Fully restart the console (not rest mode) and attempt the update download again. This clears temporary files that may interfere.

fixSteps.title: Delete and reinstall the game
fixSteps.instruction: If the update repeatedly fails, delete the entire game and reinstall it. The latest update will be included in the fresh installation.

fixSteps.title: Rebuild the database
fixSteps.instruction: Enter Safe Mode and select Rebuild Database. This can fix storage corruption that prevents update installation.

whenToCallPro: If all games fail to update even after a full system software reinstallation, the internal SSD may have bad sectors requiring professional assessment.

faq.q: Can I play the game without the update?
faq.a: You may be able to play offline single-player content, but online features and multiplayer will be locked until the update is installed.

faq.q: How much free space do I need for updates?
faq.a: The PS5 requires free space equal to the current game size plus the update size for installation. Having at least 50GB free is recommended.

title: System Software Update Failed
shortDescription: The PS5 system software update could not be installed.
fullDescription:
Error code SU-101312-8 occurs when the PS5 fails to install a system software update. This can happen during an automatic update from rest mode, a manual update, or when updating via USB. The update may download successfully but fail during the installation phase.

System software updates are critical for security, stability, and new features. A failed update can leave the console in a state where it repeatedly prompts for the same update. In rare cases, a severely failed update can require Safe Mode recovery.

The error is typically caused by corrupted download data, power interruptions during installation, or insufficient storage space. It can usually be resolved by re-downloading the update or installing it via USB from Safe Mode.
symptoms: System update fails during installation
PS5 may restart and prompt for update again
Error SU-101312-8 displayed
Console may boot into Safe Mode

fixSteps.title: Retry the update
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > System > System Software > System Software Update. Delete any pending update and re-download it over a stable connection.

fixSteps.title: Update via Safe Mode
fixSteps.instruction: Boot into Safe Mode (hold power for two beeps). Select 'Update System Software' and choose 'Update Using Internet'. This bypasses any cached corrupt files.

fixSteps.title: Update via USB
fixSteps.instruction: On a computer, go to playstation.com and download the PS5 update file. Create a USB drive with the folder structure PS5/UPDATE/ and place the update file (PS5UPDATE.PUP) inside. Insert into PS5 in Safe Mode and select 'Update from USB'.
tools: USB drive (FAT32 formatted, at least 1.1GB free)

fixSteps.title: Reinstall system software
fixSteps.instruction: If updates continue to fail, download the full reinstallation file from PlayStation's website (larger file). In Safe Mode, select 'Reset PS5 (Reinstall System Software)'. Warning: this erases all data.

whenToCallPro: If the console is stuck in a boot loop or Safe Mode cannot complete the update, the SSD may need replacement. Contact PlayStation Support.

faq.q: Will reinstalling system software delete my saves?
faq.a: Yes, a full reinstall erases everything. Back up saves to PS Plus cloud storage or USB before proceeding. A standard update via USB does not erase data.

faq.q: Why does the update keep failing?
faq.a: The most common cause is a corrupted download. Using a USB drive with a freshly downloaded update file bypasses any download corruption issues.

title: Application Has Crashed (PS4)
shortDescription: A game or application has crashed on PS4.
fullDescription:
Error code CE-34878-0 is the most common crash error on the PS4 and indicates that a running application has encountered a fatal error and closed unexpectedly. It is the PS4 equivalent of the PS5's CE-108255-1 error.

This error can be triggered by bugs in the game software, corrupted save data, corrupted game installations, hard drive problems, or system software issues. It affects all PS4 models and has been reported across hundreds of different games since the console's launch.

While usually a software issue, persistent crashes across all games may indicate a hardware problem such as a failing hard drive, overheating APU, or insufficient RAM. Single-game crashes are almost always software-related.
symptoms: Game freezes and closes to home screen
Error CE-34878-0 message displayed
May offer to send crash report to Sony
Recent unsaved progress is lost

fixSteps.title: Close and restart the game
fixSteps.instruction: Press the PS button, highlight the game, press Options, and select Close Application. Relaunch the game.

fixSteps.title: Update the game and system software
fixSteps.instruction: Check for game updates (Options > Check for Update) and system updates (Settings > System Software Update). Install all available updates.

fixSteps.title: Delete and redownload the game
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Storage > Applications. Delete the game and reinstall from disc or Library. This replaces potentially corrupted files.

fixSteps.title: Back up and delete save data
fixSteps.instruction: If the crash occurs at a specific point, the save file may be corrupted. Upload saves to PS Plus cloud, delete the local save, and start a new game to test.

fixSteps.title: Rebuild database in Safe Mode
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off PS4. Hold power button 7 seconds for Safe Mode. Select Option 5: Rebuild Database.

fixSteps.title: Initialize PS4 (last resort)
fixSteps.instruction: In Safe Mode, select Option 6 or 7 to initialize. This factory resets the PS4. Back up all data first via Settings > System > Back Up and Restore.

whenToCallPro: If the error occurs on every game after initialization, the hard drive is likely failing. Consider replacing the HDD with an SSD or contact a repair service.

faq.q: Is CE-34878-0 a serious error?
faq.a: If it happens occasionally in one game, it is a minor software issue. If it happens in every game, it could indicate hardware failure.

faq.q: Can a bad hard drive cause this error?
faq.a: Yes. A failing hard drive can cause data corruption that leads to frequent CE-34878-0 crashes. Replacing the HDD with an SSD often resolves persistent issues.

title: PlayStation Store Access Error
shortDescription: Unable to access the PlayStation Store or complete a transaction.
fullDescription:
Error NP-104530-3 occurs when the PS5 cannot connect to or complete an operation on the PlayStation Store. This can affect browsing, purchasing, downloading, or redeeming codes. The error is typically server-related but can also stem from account or payment issues.

The PlayStation Store relies on PSN backend services, and this error often appears during high-traffic periods such as major game launches, sales events, or when PSN experiences partial outages. In these cases, the issue resolves itself once server load decreases.

If the error persists outside of known outages, it may be related to your account status, payment method, or regional restrictions.

causes.cause: PSN server congestion or partial outage

symptoms: PlayStation Store fails to load or shows error
Purchases do not complete
Download codes cannot be redeemed
Store content appears but checkout fails

fixSteps.title: Check PSN status
fixSteps.instruction: Visit status.playstation.com to check if the PlayStation Store service is experiencing issues.

fixSteps.title: Wait and retry
fixSteps.instruction: If PSN is under heavy load, wait 15-30 minutes and try again. Store errors during high-traffic events are temporary.

fixSteps.title: Sign out and sign back in
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Users and Accounts > Other > Sign Out. Then sign back in with your PSN credentials.

fixSteps.title: Try the web or mobile store
fixSteps.instruction: Access store.playstation.com in a web browser or use the PlayStation app on your phone. Purchases made there will appear in your console's download queue.

fixSteps.title: Update payment method
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Users and Accounts > Account > Payment and Subscriptions. Verify or re-enter your payment details. Try an alternative payment method if available.

whenToCallPro: Contact PlayStation Support if you are charged for a purchase but do not receive the content, or if the error persists for more than 24 hours outside of known outages.

faq.q: Was I charged if the purchase failed?
faq.a: If the transaction did not complete, you should not be charged. However, check your bank or PSN transaction history to confirm. Contact PlayStation Support for any discrepancies.

faq.q: Can I buy games through the website instead?
faq.a: Yes. The PlayStation Store web version at store.playstation.com allows you to purchase games that will automatically queue for download on your console.

title: PSN Service Unavailable
shortDescription: PlayStation Network services are temporarily unavailable.
fullDescription:
Error code WS-116521-6 indicates that PlayStation Network services are currently unavailable. This is almost always a server-side issue on Sony's end rather than a problem with your console or network.

The error prevents access to online features including multiplayer, the PlayStation Store, friends list, messaging, and cloud saves. It typically occurs during scheduled maintenance, unexpected outages, or periods of extremely high server demand.

There is very little a user can do to resolve this error other than wait for Sony to restore services. The best course of action is to monitor the official PSN status page for updates.

causes.cause: Extreme server load during launches or events
Local network issue preventing PSN access

symptoms: Cannot access any online PSN features
Error WS-116521-6 displayed
Friends list, messaging, and store all unavailable
Games may still be playable offline

fixSteps.title: Check PSN status page
fixSteps.instruction: Visit status.playstation.com to check which services are affected. Maintenance windows are usually announced in advance.

fixSteps.title: Wait for service restoration
fixSteps.instruction: If PSN is down, there is nothing to do on your end. Scheduled maintenance typically lasts 1-4 hours. Unplanned outages may take longer.

fixSteps.title: Restart console and network
fixSteps.instruction: If PSN status shows all services are online but you still get the error, restart your PS5 and network equipment. Then test the internet connection.

fixSteps.title: Change DNS settings
fixSteps.instruction: In rare cases, DNS issues prevent reaching PSN servers even when they are online. Switch to Google DNS (8.8.8.8 / 8.8.4.4) in Settings > Network > Set Up Internet Connection > Advanced.

whenToCallPro: This is a server-side issue. Contact PlayStation Support only if the error persists for more than 24 hours after PSN status shows all services are online.

faq.q: Is this my fault or Sony's?
faq.a: In the vast majority of cases, this is a Sony server-side issue. Your console and network are likely fine.

faq.q: Can I play games offline during a PSN outage?
faq.a: Yes. Single-player games and any game that does not require an online connection will work normally. Online multiplayer and store access will be unavailable.

title: Disc Read Error
shortDescription: The PS5 cannot read the inserted disc.
fullDescription:
Error code CE-113212-0 indicates that the PS5 disc drive is unable to read the inserted disc. This can be caused by a dirty, scratched, or damaged disc, a dirty or misaligned laser lens in the drive, or a hardware failure in the disc drive mechanism.

The PS5 uses an Ultra HD Blu-ray drive that reads both PS5 game discs and PS4 game discs. The laser must be clean and properly aligned to read the multi-layered data on these discs. Dust, smudges, or scratches on the disc surface can prevent successful reading.

If multiple discs fail to read, the issue is likely with the disc drive itself rather than with individual discs. The PS5 Digital Edition does not have a disc drive and will not encounter this error.

causes.cause: Dirty laser lens inside the drive

symptoms: Error CE-113212-0 displayed when inserting a disc
Console may make unusual grinding or clicking sounds
Game does not appear on the home screen after disc insertion
Disc spins up but is not recognized

fixSteps.title: Clean the disc
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the disc and clean it with a soft microfiber cloth, wiping from the center hole outward in straight lines (not circular). Check for scratches, cracks, or warping.

fixSteps.title: Try a different disc
fixSteps.instruction: Insert a different game disc or Blu-ray to determine if the issue is with one specific disc or the drive itself.

fixSteps.title: Restart the PS5
fixSteps.instruction: Eject the disc, fully power off the PS5 (not rest mode), wait 30 seconds, then power on and reinsert the disc.

fixSteps.title: Clean the laser lens
fixSteps.instruction: Use a Blu-ray lens cleaning disc to clean the optical laser inside the drive. These discs have small brushes that sweep the lens during playback.

fixSteps.title: Rebuild the database
fixSteps.instruction: Boot into Safe Mode and select Rebuild Database. While primarily for software issues, this can sometimes resolve disc recognition problems.

whenToCallPro: If multiple clean, undamaged discs fail to read, the disc drive laser or mechanism has failed. Sony warranty repair or a professional console repair shop is recommended.
videoSearchQuery: PlayStation CE-113212-0 disc read error fix

faq.q: Can I fix a scratched disc?
faq.a: Minor scratches can sometimes be buffed out with a disc repair kit. Deep scratches that penetrate the data layer cannot be repaired. Replacement is the only option.

faq.q: Does this error mean my disc drive is broken?
faq.a: Not necessarily. Try cleaning the disc and testing with other discs first. If only one disc causes the error, that disc is likely the problem.

title: Could Not Connect to Wi-Fi Network
shortDescription: The PS4 could not connect to the configured Wi-Fi network.
fullDescription:
Error code NW-31294-9 appears on PS4 when the console fails to connect to a Wi-Fi network. This is typically caused by incorrect Wi-Fi password, router configuration issues, signal interference, or the Wi-Fi network being out of range.

The PS4's Wi-Fi module supports 2.4GHz and 5GHz bands (on PS4 Slim and Pro). The original PS4 only supports 2.4GHz, making it more susceptible to interference from other wireless devices. The error can also appear if the router has MAC address filtering enabled or has reached its device connection limit.

This error is specific to the wireless connection. If you can use a wired Ethernet connection instead, it will bypass this issue entirely and provide a more stable gaming experience.

causes.cause: Wi-Fi signal too weak or out of range
Router blocking the PS4 connection (MAC filtering)

symptoms: Cannot connect to Wi-Fi during setup
Error NW-31294-9 displayed
Wi-Fi network may appear in list but connection fails
Other devices connect to the same network without issues

fixSteps.title: Re-enter the Wi-Fi password
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Network > Set Up Internet Connection > Use Wi-Fi > Easy. Select your network and carefully re-enter the password. Passwords are case-sensitive.

fixSteps.title: Move closer to the router
fixSteps.instruction: Reduce the distance between the PS4 and router. Remove physical obstacles like walls and metal objects. Avoid placing the PS4 in an enclosed cabinet.

fixSteps.title: Restart the router
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug your router for 30 seconds, plug it back in, and wait 2-3 minutes for it to fully boot before attempting to connect.

fixSteps.title: Use the 5GHz band
fixSteps.instruction: If you have a PS4 Slim or Pro and a dual-band router, connect to the 5GHz network instead of 2.4GHz. The 5GHz band has less interference.

fixSteps.title: Check router settings
fixSteps.instruction: Log into your router admin panel and check: MAC address filtering is disabled or PS4's MAC is whitelisted, WPA2 security is used, the SSID is broadcasting, and the device limit has not been reached.

whenToCallPro: If the PS4 cannot see any Wi-Fi networks at all, the internal Wi-Fi antenna or module may have failed and requires hardware repair.

faq.q: Why does my phone connect but the PS4 does not?
faq.a: The PS4's Wi-Fi module is less powerful than most modern phones. It may struggle with networks that are farther away or have more interference. A wired connection is recommended.

faq.q: Does the original PS4 support 5GHz Wi-Fi?
faq.a: No. The original PS4 (CUH-1000/1100/1200 series) only supports 2.4GHz Wi-Fi. Only the PS4 Slim and PS4 Pro support both 2.4GHz and 5GHz.

title: Internet Connection Test Failed (PS4)
shortDescription: The PS4 internet connection test was unable to connect.
fullDescription:
Error code CE-33986-9 occurs when the PS4 fails the internet connection test. The console can connect to the local network but cannot reach the internet. This typically indicates a problem with the router's internet connection, DNS configuration, or ISP service.

The connection test on PS4 checks three stages: Obtain IP Address, Internet Connection, and PlayStation Network. This error specifically indicates failure at the Internet Connection stage, meaning the PS4 is connected to the local network but cannot access external servers.

This is a common error that usually resolves by restarting network equipment or adjusting DNS settings.

causes.cause: Router has lost its internet connection

symptoms: Internet connection test fails
Error CE-33986-9 displayed
Can connect to local network but not internet
Other devices may also have no internet

fixSteps.title: Check if other devices have internet
fixSteps.instruction: Test the internet on a phone or computer connected to the same network. If nothing has internet, the issue is with your router or ISP, not the PS4.

fixSteps.title: Restart modem and router
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug both modem and router for 60 seconds. Plug in the modem first, wait for all lights to stabilize (about 2 minutes), then plug in the router.

fixSteps.title: Change DNS settings
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Network > Set Up Internet Connection. Choose Custom, keep other settings default, but set DNS to Manual: Primary 8.8.8.8, Secondary 8.8.4.4.

fixSteps.title: Contact your ISP
fixSteps.instruction: If other devices also cannot connect, the issue is with your internet service. Contact your ISP to check for outages or line issues in your area.

whenToCallPro: If the PS4 consistently fails internet tests while all other devices work fine on the same network, the PS4's network hardware may need repair.

faq.q: Why can I connect to Wi-Fi but not the internet?
faq.a: Connecting to Wi-Fi only means your PS4 reached your router. Internet access requires the router to have an active connection to your ISP. Restart the modem and router to re-establish that link.

faq.q: What DNS settings should I use?
faq.a: Google DNS (8.8.8.8 / 8.8.4.4) and Cloudflare DNS (1.1.1.1 / 1.0.0.1) are the most popular alternatives. They are often faster and more reliable than your ISP's default DNS.

title: PSN Account Banned or Suspended
shortDescription: Your PlayStation Network account has been suspended or banned.
fullDescription:
Error code WS-37368-7 indicates that your PlayStation Network account has been temporarily suspended or permanently banned. This prevents signing into PSN, accessing the PlayStation Store, playing online multiplayer, and using other online features.

Account suspensions are issued by Sony's moderation team for violations of the PlayStation Network Terms of Service. Common reasons include abusive behavior in messages or voice chat, cheating or exploiting in online games, fraudulent transactions, or sharing inappropriate content.

Temporary suspensions have a set duration (typically 7 days for first offenses) after which access is automatically restored. Permanent bans are issued for severe or repeated violations and cannot be reversed. Sony sends an email to the address associated with your PSN account explaining the reason and duration of the suspension.

causes.cause: Violation of PSN Terms of Service (abusive behavior)
Cheating or exploiting in online games
Account compromised and used for violations

symptoms: Cannot sign into PlayStation Network
Error WS-37368-7 displayed at sign-in
All online features unavailable
Email from PlayStation about suspension

fixSteps.title: Check your email
fixSteps.instruction: Look for an email from Sony/PlayStation at the email address linked to your PSN account. It will explain the reason for the suspension and its duration.

fixSteps.title: Wait for temporary suspension to expire
fixSteps.instruction: If it is a temporary suspension, you must wait until the specified date for access to be restored. There is no way to end a suspension early.

fixSteps.title: Appeal the suspension
fixSteps.instruction: If you believe the suspension was issued in error, contact PlayStation Support through their website and submit an appeal. Provide your PSN ID and explain why you believe the action was incorrect.

fixSteps.title: Secure your account if compromised
fixSteps.instruction: If your account was used by someone else, enable two-factor authentication immediately after regaining access. Go to Settings > Users and Accounts > Security > 2-Step Verification.

repairTime: Varies (7 days for typical first suspension)
whenToCallPro: Contact PlayStation Support if you believe the suspension is in error or if your account was compromised.

faq.q: Can I create a new account to bypass the ban?
faq.a: You can create a new PSN account, but you will lose access to all purchases, trophies, and friends on the banned account. Console bans (different from account bans) may prevent any account from going online on that specific console.

faq.q: How do I avoid getting banned?
faq.a: Follow the PSN Terms of Service: do not send abusive messages, do not cheat in online games, do not issue chargebacks for PSN purchases, and do not share inappropriate content.

title: System Software Update Failed (PS4)
shortDescription: The PS4 system software update could not be installed.
fullDescription:
Error code SU-42481-9 occurs on the PS4 when a system software update fails to download or install. This is the PS4 equivalent of the PS5's SU-101312-8 error and prevents the console from updating to the latest firmware version.

The error can occur during automatic updates from rest mode, manual updates via the internet, or USB updates. Common causes include corrupted downloads, unstable internet connections, and insufficient hard drive space.

System updates are required for many online features and games. If the console cannot update normally, Safe Mode provides alternative update methods including USB installation.
symptoms: System update fails during download or installation
Error SU-42481-9 displayed
Console prompts for the same update repeatedly
May not be able to access online features

fixSteps.title: Delete the update notification
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Notifications, highlight the update notification, press Options, and select Delete. Then try the update again from Settings > System Software Update.

fixSteps.title: Free up hard drive space
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Storage > System Storage and delete unused games, apps, or captures. At least 3-4GB of free space is needed for system updates.

fixSteps.title: Update via Safe Mode
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off PS4. Hold power button for 7 seconds until second beep. Connect controller via USB. Select Option 3: Update System Software > Update Using Internet.

fixSteps.title: Update via USB in Safe Mode
fixSteps.instruction: Download the update file from playstation.com on a computer. Place it on a USB drive in the folder path PS4/UPDATE/PS4UPDATE.PUP. In Safe Mode, select Update from USB Storage Device.

fixSteps.title: Initialize PS4 with reinstallation
fixSteps.instruction: If all else fails, download the full reinstallation file from PlayStation's website and use Safe Mode Option 7. This erases all data and reinstalls the system software from scratch.

whenToCallPro: If the PS4 cannot complete a system update even via USB with the full reinstallation file, the hard drive may need replacement.

faq.q: Can I use my PS4 without the latest update?
faq.a: You can use offline features, but online multiplayer, the PlayStation Store, and other PSN features require the latest system software.

faq.q: Why does the update fail every time?
faq.a: The most reliable fix is updating via USB in Safe Mode. This avoids download corruption and network issues entirely.

title: PlayStation Plus Required
shortDescription: A PlayStation Plus subscription is required for this feature.
fullDescription:
Error code NP-34993-8 appears when you attempt to access a feature that requires an active PlayStation Plus subscription. Online multiplayer for most games, monthly free games, cloud save storage, and exclusive discounts all require PS Plus.

This error can appear if your subscription has expired, your payment method failed to renew the subscription, or if you are trying to use a feature that was not included in the PS Plus tier you are subscribed to (Essential, Extra, or Premium).

Some free-to-play games like Fortnite and Apex Legends do not require PS Plus for online play. If you get this error in a free-to-play game, the issue may be with your account settings rather than your subscription.

causes.cause: Auto-renewal failed due to payment issue
Feature requires a higher PS Plus tier

symptoms: Cannot access online multiplayer
Error NP-34993-8 displayed
Free monthly games are locked
Cloud saves unavailable

fixSteps.title: Check subscription status
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Users and Accounts > Account > Subscriptions. Check if your PS Plus membership is active and when it expires.

fixSteps.title: Renew or subscribe to PS Plus
fixSteps.instruction: If your subscription has expired, go to the PlayStation Store and purchase a PS Plus plan. Choose Essential for online multiplayer, Extra for game catalog access, or Premium for classic games and streaming.

fixSteps.title: Update payment method
fixSteps.instruction: If auto-renewal failed, go to Settings > Users and Accounts > Account > Payment and Subscriptions. Update your credit card or add PlayStation Store wallet funds.

fixSteps.title: Restore licenses
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Users and Accounts > Other > Restore Licenses. This refreshes your account entitlements and can fix subscription recognition issues.

whenToCallPro: Contact PlayStation Support if you have an active subscription but continue to receive this error after restoring licenses.
videoSearchQuery: PlayStation NP-34993-8 PS Plus required fix

faq.q: Do all games require PS Plus for online play?
faq.a: No. Free-to-play games like Fortnite, Apex Legends, Genshin Impact, and Rocket League do not require PS Plus for online multiplayer.

faq.q: What are the PS Plus tiers?
faq.a: PS Plus Essential includes online multiplayer and monthly games. PS Plus Extra adds a catalog of downloadable games. PS Plus Premium adds classic games and cloud streaming.

title: Payment or Wallet Error
shortDescription: A payment processing error occurred on the PlayStation Store.
fullDescription:
Error code E-8210604A is a payment processing error that occurs when attempting to make a purchase on the PlayStation Store or add funds to your PSN wallet. The transaction cannot be completed due to issues with the payment method, billing information, or Sony's payment processing system.

This error is commonly triggered by expired credit cards, insufficient funds, billing address mismatches, or banks blocking the transaction. It can also occur during PSN payment server issues that temporarily prevent all transactions.

The error can be frustrating during time-limited sales, but it is almost always resolvable by updating payment information or using an alternative payment method such as PSN gift cards.

causes.cause: Billing address mismatch with card on file
Account wallet or purchase limit reached

symptoms: Purchase fails on PlayStation Store
Error E-8210604A displayed at checkout
Cannot add funds to wallet
Subscription renewal fails

fixSteps.title: Check payment method
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Users and Accounts > Account > Payment and Subscriptions > Payment Methods. Verify your card has not expired and the billing information matches exactly.

fixSteps.title: Remove and re-add your card
fixSteps.instruction: Delete the existing payment method and add it again with current, accurate billing details. Ensure the card number, expiration, CVV, and billing address are all correct.

fixSteps.title: Contact your bank
fixSteps.instruction: Your bank may be blocking the transaction as a fraud precaution. Call your bank and authorize transactions to Sony Interactive Entertainment.

fixSteps.title: Use PSN gift cards
fixSteps.instruction: Purchase PSN wallet cards from a retail store or online retailer. Redeem the code on the PlayStation Store to add funds, then use wallet balance for the purchase.

fixSteps.title: Try the web store
fixSteps.instruction: Access store.playstation.com in a browser and attempt the purchase there. The web store sometimes processes payments when the console store does not.

whenToCallPro: Contact PlayStation Support if the error persists across all payment methods including wallet funds, as there may be an account-level billing hold.

faq.q: Was my card charged even though the purchase failed?
faq.a: Typically no. However, some banks may show a temporary hold that disappears within 3-5 business days. Check your bank statement.

faq.q: Why does my bank keep blocking PlayStation purchases?
faq.a: International transactions or unusual purchase patterns can trigger fraud detection. Call your bank to whitelist Sony Interactive Entertainment as a trusted merchant.

title: Cannot Access Hard Drive or USB Storage
shortDescription: The PS4 cannot access the internal hard drive or connected USB storage device.
fullDescription:
Error code CE-30005-8 is a storage access error on the PS4 that occurs when the console cannot read from the internal hard drive or an external USB storage device. This is often a sign of hard drive failure, data corruption, or a problem with the USB connection for external storage.

When this error occurs, games may fail to launch, saves may be inaccessible, and downloads may stall. The error can appear intermittently (suggesting early-stage drive failure) or consistently (indicating more severe corruption or hardware damage).

This is one of the more serious PS4 errors as it often points to hardware degradation. The original PS4 uses a mechanical hard drive that can fail over time due to wear, physical shock, or overheating.

causes.cause: Data corruption on the hard drive
Faulty USB cable or port for external storage
External hard drive not properly formatted

symptoms: Games fail to launch or crash during loading
Error CE-30005-8 displayed
Downloads fail or stall
Console may be unusually slow
Clicking sounds from hard drive area

fixSteps.title: Restart the PS4
fixSteps.instruction: Fully power off the PS4 (not rest mode), wait 30 seconds, and power it back on. Transient read errors may clear after a restart.

fixSteps.title: Check external storage connection
fixSteps.instruction: If using an external drive, disconnect and reconnect the USB cable. Try a different USB port. Test with a different USB cable if available.

fixSteps.title: Rebuild the database
fixSteps.instruction: Boot into Safe Mode (hold power 7 seconds). Select Option 5: Rebuild Database. This scans and repairs file system errors on the hard drive.

fixSteps.title: Back up data and replace the hard drive
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, the hard drive is likely failing. Back up all possible data via Settings > System > Back Up and Restore. Remove the PS4's top cover, slide out the HDD caddy, and replace the 2.5-inch drive with a new HDD or SSD. Reinstall system software via USB.

whenToCallPro: If you are not comfortable replacing the hard drive yourself, or if the console shows additional hardware symptoms like overheating or graphical issues, seek professional repair.
videoSearchQuery: PlayStation CE-30005-8 hard drive error fix

faq.q: Can I upgrade to an SSD?
faq.a: Yes, and it is highly recommended. Replacing the PS4's mechanical HDD with an SSD significantly improves load times, reduces noise, and eliminates mechanical failure risk. Any 2.5-inch SATA SSD will work.

faq.q: Will I lose all my data?
faq.a: If the hard drive is failing, some data may already be inaccessible. Back up everything possible before replacing the drive. Cloud saves via PS Plus are the safest backup method.

title: Internet Browser Cannot Display Page
shortDescription: The PS4 internet browser cannot load the requested web page.
fullDescription:
Error code WV-33898-1 occurs when the PS4's built-in web browser fails to load a web page. This can be due to network connectivity issues, DNS resolution failures, or the page being incompatible with the PS4's limited browser engine.

The PS4's web browser has limited JavaScript support and may struggle with modern, complex websites. It does not support many modern web standards, plugins, or content types. This error can also appear when the page requires authentication or redirects that the browser cannot handle.

While the browser is useful for basic web tasks and logging into captive portal Wi-Fi networks (like hotels), it is not designed for regular web browsing.
symptoms: Web page fails to load in PS4 browser
Error WV-33898-1 displayed
Page may partially load before failing
Some websites work while others do not

fixSteps.title: Try a different website
fixSteps.instruction: Visit a simple website like google.com to determine if the issue is with a specific site or with all internet access.

fixSteps.title: Clear browser data
fixSteps.instruction: In the browser, press Options > Settings > Delete Cookies and Website Data. This clears cached data that may interfere with page loading.

fixSteps.title: Test internet connection
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Network > Test Internet Connection to verify the PS4 has internet access. Fix any connection issues first.

fixSteps.title: Change DNS settings
fixSteps.instruction: Set custom DNS in Settings > Network > Set Up Internet Connection > Custom. Use Google DNS (8.8.8.8 / 8.8.4.4) for better name resolution.

whenToCallPro: This is not a hardware issue. If you need full web browsing capabilities, use a dedicated device like a phone, tablet, or computer.

faq.q: Why can't the PS4 browser load many websites?
faq.a: The PS4 browser is very basic and does not support modern web standards. It is designed primarily for captive portal login (hotel Wi-Fi) and simple browsing. Use a phone or computer for complex websites.

faq.q: Does the PS5 have a better browser?
faq.a: The PS5 does not have a dedicated browser application. You can access a basic browser through the system's messaging or help links, but Sony has not included a standalone browser app.

title: Downloaded Data May Be Corrupted
shortDescription: The PS4 has detected that downloaded content may be corrupted.
fullDescription:
Error code CE-32930-7 indicates that the PS4 has detected potential data corruption in a downloaded game, application, or update. The corrupted data cannot be used and must be re-downloaded.

This error typically occurs when a download is interrupted by a power outage, network disconnection, or system crash. It can also occur when the hard drive has bad sectors that corrupt data as it is written. In most cases, simply deleting the corrupt data and re-downloading resolves the issue.

If this error occurs frequently, it may indicate that the PS4's hard drive is beginning to fail, especially if the console is several years old. Hard drives are mechanical components with limited lifespans, and data corruption is often an early sign of failure.

causes.cause: Download interrupted by power loss or network drop
Hard drive bad sectors corrupting written data

symptoms: Error CE-32930-7 displayed
Downloaded game or update cannot be installed
Content appears in downloads but is unusable
May see 'cannot continue downloading' messages

fixSteps.title: Delete the corrupted download
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Notifications > Downloads. Find the corrupted file, press Options, and select Delete. Then go to Library and re-download the content.

fixSteps.title: Use a wired connection for download
fixSteps.instruction: Connect an Ethernet cable to avoid Wi-Fi drops during the download. Wireless interruptions are a common cause of download corruption.

fixSteps.title: Disable rest mode downloads temporarily
fixSteps.instruction: Keep the PS4 fully powered on during the download instead of using rest mode. Go to Settings > Power Saving Settings > Set Features Available in Rest Mode and temporarily disable Stay Connected to the Internet.

fixSteps.title: Rebuild database
fixSteps.instruction: Boot into Safe Mode and select Rebuild Database. This repairs file system issues that may cause download corruption.

fixSteps.title: Test and replace hard drive
fixSteps.instruction: If downloads are repeatedly corrupted, the hard drive may have bad sectors. Back up data and replace the drive with a new SSD.

whenToCallPro: If data corruption errors are frequent across multiple downloads, the hard drive is likely failing and should be replaced by a professional if you are not comfortable doing it yourself.

faq.q: Will I lose my save data?
faq.a: No. This error affects downloaded content (games, updates), not save data. Your game saves are stored separately on the hard drive.

faq.q: Why do my downloads keep getting corrupted?
faq.a: If it happens once, it was likely a network or power glitch. If it happens repeatedly, the hard drive may be developing bad sectors and should be replaced.

title: PSN Sign-In Failed
shortDescription: Unable to sign into PlayStation Network.
fullDescription:
Error code NP-31952-0 occurs when the PS4 cannot sign into PlayStation Network. This prevents access to online multiplayer, the PlayStation Store, friends list, and other PSN features. The error can be caused by server issues, network problems, or account-related issues.

Unlike the WS-37368-7 ban error, NP-31952-0 is typically a connectivity or server issue rather than an account enforcement action. It frequently appears during PSN maintenance periods or when the sign-in servers are under heavy load.

The error can also be triggered by two-factor authentication issues, expired passwords, or conflicts when the same account is signed in on multiple consoles.

causes.cause: PSN sign-in server maintenance or outage
Account signed in on another console

symptoms: Cannot sign into PSN from the home screen
Error NP-31952-0 displayed
Sign-in attempt times out or fails immediately
Offline mode indicator appears

fixSteps.title: Check PSN status
fixSteps.instruction: Visit status.playstation.com to check if Account Management and Sign-In services are operational.

fixSteps.title: Test internet connection
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Network > Test Internet Connection. Ensure IP Address and Internet Connection both pass before checking PSN.

fixSteps.title: Sign out and sign back in
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Account Management > Sign Out. Wait 30 seconds, then sign back in with your credentials.

fixSteps.title: Reset your password
fixSteps.instruction: If you suspect a password issue, visit account.sonyentertainmentnetwork.com on a phone or computer to reset your PSN password.

fixSteps.title: Remove and re-add account
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Login Settings > User Management > Delete User. Then create a new user and sign in with your PSN account. This resets the local account data.

whenToCallPro: Contact PlayStation Support if you cannot sign in after all troubleshooting steps and PSN services are confirmed online.
videoSearchQuery: PlayStation NP-31952-0 sign in error fix

faq.q: Is my account banned if I get this error?
faq.a: No, NP-31952-0 is a connectivity error, not a ban. Account bans show error WS-37368-7 specifically.

faq.q: Can I be signed in on two consoles at once?
faq.a: You can have one primary PS4 and sign in on one other PS4, but active sessions can sometimes conflict. Sign out of other consoles if you experience sign-in issues.
